profile.name: Elizabeth
profile.role_in_household: Primary client; higher W-2 earner and household organizer; focuses on budgeting, retirement readiness, and keeping plans realistic.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate-to-high: comfortable with budgeting and retirement accounts; asks for plain-English explanations on taxes, dividends/interest, and how accounts work together.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Generally growth-oriented for long horizons, but wants guardrails; will accept volatility if the plan shows cash reserves and a clear retirement path.
profile.communication_style: Measured and structured; tends to cite exact figures she’s confident about (income, monthly expenses) and uses hedges like "I think" when unsure. Will pause to confirm terms (taxable vs retirement, gross vs net).
emotional_tendencies: mild anxiety when assumptions feel fuzzy
relief when there are written next steps
profile.decision_making_style: Deliberate: prefers comparing 2–3 options, then deciding with a checklist and timeline.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously trusting: open to guidance but expects transparency on fees, taxes, and rationale.
internal_biases: loss aversion around near-term cash needs
hidden_concerns: worries she carries most of the planning burden
concerned about being in a high tax bracket and "doing something wrong" tax-wise

profile.name: Emily
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; business-owner mindset; more engaged with investing/income-producing assets and tends to steer portfolio conversations.
profile.financial_literacy_level: High on investing concepts, uneven on planning mechanics: understands brokerage income and alternatives; sometimes mixes up balance vs income or gross vs net and prefers summaries.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Comfortable taking risk and holding higher-volatility positions; tends to focus on long-term return potential and may downplay drawdowns unless liquidity is threatened.
profile.communication_style: Fast, conversational, big-picture; speaks in round numbers ("two hundred-ish") and may interrupt with a thesis. Uses fillers ("so," "yeah") and asks for the bottom line first, details second.
profile.decision_making_style: Thesis-driven and quick: decides once the strategy aligns with her worldview; prefers simple rules and periodic check-ins.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Selective trust: engages when the advisor demonstrates tax awareness and portfolio construction skill; skeptical of generic or overly conservative advice.
internal_biases: anchoring to prior highs or target yield
hidden_concerns: doesn’t want to be judged for lower earned income relative to investment income
quiet worry about liquidity and access to cash if markets drop

Advisor: Agreed. Coming back to the plan: you said earlier you want it realistic and not fuzzy. Did I get those right?
